# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.
# Category: kong_no_auth

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1  # use the correct version of Kubernetes resource used in your context, usually 'networking' or 'apiextensions', depending on how you define configurations (e.g., via an API Extension like OpenAPI) within a Pod
kind: KongAPIConfigurations  
metadata:    # Metadata contains details about this particular configuration - name and namespace for Kubernetes in most cases, but may vary by resource type – e.g.. service discovery using Consul or DNS if not managed at the API extension level etc., which can be useful when troubleshooting issues around specific configurations
  annotations:   # Contains custom user metadata that could represent additional details about this configuration for other uses, like source of truth where it came from... e.g.. creation timestamp and creator name or ID (username) if the config was created by a service/controller based on Kubernetes events etc
    annotation-key1: annoValue   # Replace with your own metadata keys & values for customization purposes, they can be named anything you like in this structure. This is how information about creation or source of configuration will travel when debugging related issues – it's a good idea to define the names and purpose here
    annotation-key2: annoValue   # Replace with your own metadata keys & values for customization purposes, they can be named anything you like in this structure. This is how information about creation or source of configuration will travel when debugging related issues – it's a good idea to define the names and purpose here
spec:    # Contains details defining configurations - what services are being exposed (routes), which upstream targets they proxy requests too, etc., these can be defined at different points depending on use case if not controlled via an API extension like OpenAPI – this is where you should define your APIs / Microservices endpoints
   hosts:     # The name of the configuration - unique per Kong plugin (e.g.. HTTPBASICAUTH) in most cases, but can be reused with different plugins if required by specific use case or Kubernetes-specific resources – this is where you could set host information for your APIs
    service1:   # Service names and the associated configuration details (what upstream targets to proxy requests too etc.) - replace these placeholders as per requirements, it should be unique within KongPlugin definition which can then point at all services or routes using a specific API Gateway configurations. – e.g.. 'service_name': {'targets' : ['upstream1', ...],...}
      targets:   # List of upsteam service names the gateway will proxy requests to for that micro-services endpoints - each should be unique per KongPlugin definition (e.g., HTTPBASICAUTH, etc.) – e.g.. '['upstream1', ...]' which refers back what host has a defined configuration in your Kubernetes
status:    # Contains the status of this particular API Gateway plugin - enabled/disabled and any other configurations related details (e.g., whether to use SSL for all requests, etc.) – e.g.. 'enabled': true }' which shows if it is currently active or not in KongAPIConfigurations resource
